mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Grafana instances and their resources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer managing resources via custom resources (CRs) and infrastructure as code methodologies. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes CRs and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Grafana Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator using Helm, specifying the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator grafana/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step involves creating a custom resource definition (CRD) for Grafana, which defines the configuration for your Grafana instance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with basic configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Displays the details of the Grafana instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Grafana instances are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator.
> Deletes the Grafana instance, which may result in data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana configurations. This process involves draining the Grafana instance, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator grafana/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backs up the current Grafana configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Grafana instance is running with the new version.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not have been created or may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana CRD is applied correctly and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the Grafana pod status with 'kubectl get pods' and logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not allow access to the Grafana resources.

**Fix:**
Review and update the RBAC roles and bindings for the Grafana Operator.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Invalid configuration'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The configuration provided in the Grafana CRD may have syntax errors.

**Fix:**
Validate the YAML syntax and ensure all required fields are correctly specified.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new Grafana features, including enhanced dashboard management and improved API interactions.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get grafana grafana'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
